completed_phases.title: macOS solo protocols and local shell

upcoming_phases.title: Live GitHub release workflow verification
upcoming_phases.rationale: The hosted release workflow now has a shared secret-contract tool and a branch-safe preview path, but it still needs real signing and notarization secret values before a live run can progress past preflight.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Multi-surface regression hardening
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: Browser mode and native mode still use different transport stacks, so long-lived regression coverage needs a more explicit cross-surface matrix than the current browser validate path plus native fixtures.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Local fixture sandbox hardening
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The broader localhost SSH fixtures produce valid results when run unsandboxed, but desktop sandboxing still obscures them during local validation and should be hardened into a documented, lower-friction path.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Notarization and release promotion
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The packaging phase now produces signed release artifacts and logs, but the macOS bundle still fails Gatekeeper assessment until notarization, stapling, and promotion are automated.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Release credential portability hardening
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The release flow is now notarized and promoted locally, but it still depends on a machine-local `notarytool` keychain profile and has no CI-backed promotion credential path.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Live GitHub release workflow verification
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The repository now has a GitHub-hosted release workflow, and the verification branch has executed it live; the remaining blocker is provisioning the required secrets so the run can proceed past preflight.

risk_remediation_phases.title: GitHub release secret provisioning
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The hosted release workflow now shares one audited secret contract and deterministic preflight, but GitHub still lacks the required secrets and the local workstation does not expose exportable signing/notary material for automated provisioning.

tasks.title: Move native key inspection, generation, and known-host scanning into Tauri
Add native trust and key regression coverage plus repo validation updates
Update docs, roadmap, validation log, bootstrap, and shared state
Add packaging, signing, notarization, and release diagnostics hardening
Automate notarization, stapling, and release promotion for the macOS bundle
Expand regression coverage across browser and native transport surfaces
Move release credentials and promotion into a portable CI-backed release path
Reduce local desktop sandbox friction for macOS localhost SSH fixtures
Add stable vault metadata to local snapshot exports and imports
Add snapshot ancestry and import preview classification to local vault bundles
Design and implement record-level merge semantics for vault snapshots
Handle record deletions and user-resolvable conflicts for vault snapshot merges
Model deletions and tombstones for vault snapshot merges
Define the remote vault exchange contract for encrypted snapshot sync
Add tombstone retention and compaction rules for vault snapshot merges
Define key material rotation and authentication rules for remote vault envelopes
Persist and apply trusted remote sync key policies at sync boundaries
Expose trusted remote sync key policies for operator bootstrap and management
Run the GitHub-hosted macOS release workflow with live repository secrets
Provision required GitHub signing and notarization secrets for the hosted macOS release workflow
tasks.blocked_reason: GitHub has no configured release secrets and the local workstation only exposes the Developer ID identity, not exportable certificate/password or notarization credentials.

tasks.title: Upgrade GitHub-hosted actions to native Node 24 targets where available
Normalize canonical roadmap, shared state, agents, and prompts for bootstrap execution
Install runtime guardrail validation and record bootstrap evidence
Enforce the runtime guardrail validator on pull requests
Add shared GitHub release secret audit, validation, and apply tooling
Capture exportable signing certificate material and notarization credentials for GitHub secret provisioning
Make hosts protocol-aware across inventory, filters, and session creation
Launch a real native local shell session through the Tauri PTY bridge
Hide or disable SSH-only features for non-SSH protocols across host and session UI
Implement executable telnet, serial, and mosh transports on top of the protocol-aware host model
Detect missing native telnet, serial, and mosh client prerequisites before runtime launch
Add live validation coverage for telnet, serial, and mosh runtime paths
Persist searchable session command history for app-dispatched commands
Capture searchable session output excerpts alongside command history
Upgrade snippets into parameterized structured runbooks with execution records

risks.title: Native trust and key tooling still proxied through the backend
risks.summary: Native mode previously proxied key inspection, key generation, and known-host scans through the Node backend.

risks.title: Browser and native transport paths diverge intentionally
risks.summary: Cross-surface runtime coverage now exercises both browser and native transport seams through targeted unit tests, the shared Vitest suite runs on macOS CI, and browser e2e plus native fixtures remain in the validation matrix.

risks.title: Local macOS SSH fixtures need an unsandboxed shell
risks.summary: Native trust and transport fixture scripts now run an explicit localhost sshd preflight before Cargo tests, so unsupported shells fail fast with deterministic guidance instead of partial SSH fixture errors.

risks.title: Native bundle still used a placeholder macOS bundle identifier
risks.summary: The signed bundle and release manifest still emitted `com.example.terminalworkspace`, which is invalid release metadata for a real macOS package.

risks.title: Signed macOS bundle is not notarized
risks.summary: The local release path now submits the signed archive to Apple, staples the accepted ticket, and produces a Gatekeeper-accepted app bundle plus a promoted stable manifest.

risks.title: Release automation still requires a machine-local notary profile
risks.summary: Release automation now supports App Store Connect key auth, Apple ID auth, and local profile fallback, and the repo includes CI-backed signing/notarization/publish workflow plus documented env contracts.

risks.title: GitHub release workflow has not been executed with live repository secrets
risks.summary: The GitHub-hosted release workflow now runs on the verification branch and reaches the hosted preflight gate on Actions.

risks.title: GitHub-hosted release workflow is missing signing and notarization secrets
risks.summary: The live hosted run on Actions now fails deterministically in the release-secret preflight because `MACOS_CERTIFICATE_P12_BASE64`, `MACOS_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD`, `MACOS_KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD`, `MACOS_SIGN_IDENTITY`, and all notarization credential paths are unset in GitHub.

risks.title: Pinned GitHub actions still target the deprecated Node 20 runtime
risks.summary: The workflows now use the current action majors (`actions/checkout@v6`, `actions/setup-node@v6`, `actions/upload-artifact@v7`, and `pnpm/action-setup@v5`), and the hosted release run on the modernization branch no longer emits the Node 20 runtime deprecation annotation.

risks.title: Vault imports still replace the local workspace atomically
risks.summary: Same-vault imports now preview merge impact and can merge non-conflicting hosts, keys, snippets, and known-host entries instead of overwriting unrelated local records.

risks.title: Vault merges do not yet model deletions or user-resolvable conflicts
risks.summary: Same-vault imports now carry tombstone deletions, remove superseded local records during merge, and let timestamp-tied conflicts keep local or prefer imported state.

risks.title: Vault tombstones have no retention or compaction policy
risks.summary: Deletion tombstones are now deduplicated, age-limited, and capped per entity kind before persistence, import analysis, and local export, preventing unbounded local snapshot growth.

risks.title: Remote vault envelopes do not yet define key rotation or signer trust rules
risks.summary: Remote vault envelopes now have an explicit trust policy model with allowed vault scopes, key validity windows, rotation thresholds, retirement cutoffs, and replacement key IDs.

risks.title: Remote sync trust policies are not yet persisted or enforced by runtime workflows
risks.summary: Remote sync trust policies now persist in a dedicated store and the runtime envelope inspection path loads local vault context plus that policy automatically before accepting envelopes.

risks.title: Trusted sync key policies have no operator management or bootstrap surface
risks.summary: Settings now exposes trusted sync key import/export, allow-unknown-key policy control, explicit trusted-key edits, and operator bootstrap distribution for persisted remote sync trust rules.

risks.title: Local workstation does not expose exportable release secret material
risks.summary: The repo can detect the local Developer ID identity, but there is no P12 bundle, certificate password, keychain password, or notarization credential material in env files or accessible local export paths, so automated GitHub secret provisioning cannot complete from this machine state alone.

risks.title: Telnet, serial, and mosh are modeled but not executable yet
risks.summary: Telnet, serial, and mosh now launch through the native PTY bridge with protocol-aware UI and host defaults instead of stopping at inventory only.

risks.title: Native telnet, serial, and mosh sessions depend on local client binaries
risks.summary: The desktop app now checks native client availability before session launch and surfaces inline install guidance in the host editor and terminal pane instead of failing later in the spawn path.

risks.title: Telnet, serial, and mosh runtime paths do not yet have live end-to-end validation fixtures
risks.summary: Live protocol fixtures now drive telnet, serial, and mosh through the native PTY launch path with deterministic client overrides, and the native transport script runs that coverage before the broader localhost SSH fixture.

risks.title: Session history currently covers app-dispatched commands only
risks.summary: The first session-history slice intentionally records only commands launched through app-managed dispatch paths so interactive prompts and password entry are not captured, but manual terminal input and output excerpts are not yet persisted.

opportunities.title: Convert native parity into release-ready packaging
opportunities.summary: The native shell now produces signed bundles, versioned release archives, machine-readable manifests, and CI preview artifacts.

opportunities.title: Design encrypted vault and sync architecture
opportunities.summary: Local vault snapshots now carry stable lineage metadata, same-vault imports merge and delete records before apply, tombstones compact automatically, remote exchange envelopes include persisted trusted-key policy enforcement with digest verification plus rotation and retirement rules, and operators can now import, export, and manage trusted sync keys in Settings.

opportunities.title: Publish promoted notarized artifacts through CI-backed release channels
opportunities.summary: The repo now includes a CI release workflow plus a GitHub publish script, the workflow has been executed live on GitHub Actions, and the remaining step is provisioning release secrets so the hosted path can complete.

opportunities.title: Keep workflow dependencies aligned with current hosted action runtimes
opportunities.summary: The workflows now run on current major action releases and no longer emit the Node 20 hosted runtime deprecation warning during live GitHub Actions execution.

opportunities.title: Use protocol-aware hosts and a native local shell as the solo-macOS foundation
opportunities.summary: Hosts now carry explicit protocol metadata and the native bridge can launch a real local login shell, which establishes the foundation for local terminal workflows, runbooks, session history, and broader protocol parity on top of one inventory model.

opportunities.title: Use the persisted Sessions history surface as the base for richer runbook evidence
opportunities.summary: Sessions now exposes a searchable persisted command history rail with replay actions, which gives output capture, runbook evidence, and safer history semantics a real user-facing surface to build on.

incidents.summary: The first validate pass failed because Playwright still expected pre-declutter host headings and the old Settings runtime copy.
Local macOS sshd fixtures failed inside the desktop sandbox; rerunning the native trust and transport suites unsandboxed produced valid passing results.
The signed packaging gate originally reported `spctl: not_accepted`, but the notarization phase resolved it by submitting the archive to Apple, stapling the ticket, and revalidating with Gatekeeper.
The first signed release rebuild surfaced stale forward/snippet test payloads and a reused Response test harness bug; both were fixed before the final validation pass.
The hosted release workflow initially parsed into zero-job failures because the certificate import heredoc was not valid YAML; the workflow now parses locally and runs real jobs on GitHub Actions.
The first real hosted release runs now fail in a deterministic preflight because the required signing and notarization secrets are not configured in GitHub.

The initial live hosted release verification emitted Node 20 deprecation annotations from pinned action versions; upgrading to the latest major action releases removed the warning on the modernization-branch run.

decisions.title: Move native trust and key tooling into Tauri
decisions.summary: Native mode now handles key inspection, key generation, and known-host scanning in Rust via Tauri commands and system OpenSSH tooling, while browser mode keeps the backend path.

decisions.title: Split native validation by scope
decisions.summary: Keep `native:key` as the fast key regression, `native:trust` as the phase gate, and `native:fixtures` as the broader localhost transport suite that requires explicit unsandboxed execution.

decisions.title: Make macOS packaging explicit and machine-readable
decisions.summary: Use an explicit `MACOS_SIGN_MODE=require` path for local signed bundles, a `skip` preview path for CI artifact generation, and emit release manifests plus verification logs into `artifacts/release/`.

decisions.title: Automate local notarization and stable promotion
decisions.summary: Drive notarization with a local `notarytool` keychain profile, staple and validate the accepted app bundle, then promote the notarized artifact into a stable-channel directory with manifest and checksum outputs.

decisions.title: Prefer portable notary auth and CI-backed release publishing
decisions.summary: Release automation now resolves App Store Connect key auth first, Apple ID second, and local keychain profiles last, then promotes notarized artifacts into a GitHub-release-ready asset set and CI workflow.

decisions.title: Preflight localhost fixture capability before Cargo SSH suites
decisions.summary: The native trust and transport scripts now validate temporary localhost sshd startup and host scanning before running the Cargo fixtures so local failures are explicit and reproducible.

decisions.title: Carry stable vault metadata in local snapshot exports
decisions.summary: The exported local snapshot format now includes a shared vault identifier, source device identifier, and snapshot identifier so future sync work can build on stable identities instead of a flat config dump.

decisions.title: Verify hosted macOS release on a dedicated phase branch
decisions.summary: The hosted release workflow now runs on the dedicated verification branch, synthesizes a preview tag/channel for branch pushes, and publishes against the branch SHA so GitHub Actions can be exercised safely before a default-branch release.

decisions.title: Fail hosted release runs before signing when secrets are missing
decisions.summary: The GitHub-hosted release workflow now validates signing and notarization secrets in a dedicated preflight step and only uploads artifacts when release artifacts actually exist.

decisions.title: Use current major GitHub action releases in all workflows
decisions.summary: The CI, validation, and hosted release workflows now use `actions/checkout@v6`, `actions/setup-node@v6`, `actions/upload-artifact@v7`, and `pnpm/action-setup@v5` so hosted runs no longer emit the Node 20 runtime deprecation annotation.

decisions.title: Make hosts protocol-aware and ship a native local shell first
decisions.summary: Hosts now carry explicit protocol metadata across inventory and session state, the native shell can launch a real macOS login shell through the Tauri PTY bridge, and SSH-only features remain gated behind protocol-aware UI guardrails.

decisions.title: Run telnet, serial, and mosh through the native PTY bridge
decisions.summary: Telnet, serial, and mosh sessions now launch as native external commands under the Tauri PTY bridge, while SSH-only features remain gated and serial baud rates are preserved without collapsing into SSH port assumptions.

decisions.title: Preflight native protocol clients before session launch
decisions.summary: The desktop app now resolves telnet, mosh, screen, and cu availability through a shared Tauri runtime-status command and surfaces any missing-client guidance inline before connect attempts.

decisions.title: Validate non-SSH protocol launches with deterministic native fixture clients
decisions.summary: Native telnet, serial, and mosh validation now uses command-path overrides to point the PTY launch path at fixture executables, so the repo can exercise live protocol sessions without depending on workstation-installed services or remote endpoints.

decisions.title: Start session history with app-dispatched commands only
decisions.summary: The first session-history slice persists queued and replayed commands only, which gives Sessions a real searchable history surface without capturing arbitrary interactive input before redaction and output rules exist.

environment_status.native_protocol_runtime: passed (vitest + desktop build + cargo test + live protocol fixtures)
environment_status.native_protocol_live_validation: passed (cargo external protocol fixture + native transport script)
environment_status.session_command_history: passed (vitest + desktop build + cargo test + validator)
